cover:
A highly detailed, technical blueprint-style developer infographic poster on the theme of "SOQL OPTIMIZATION: THE MISSING GUIDE".
Overall Theme: High-tech dark mode developer dashboard, cybersecurity blueprint, with sharp glowing lines and neat borders.
Colors: Deep dark navy background with glowing neon blue, electric cyan, warning red, and success green highlights.

Header:
- A prominent top-center glowing blue header: "SOQL OPTIMIZATION: THE MISSING GUIDE".
- Below it, a clean subtitle: "Most SOQL Problems Are Data Modeling Problems in an Apex Costume" with a small Salesforce logo on the top-left and an "APEX BEST PRACTICES" badge on the top-right.

Section 1: "WHY IT MATTERS" (Top Row, 3 Horizontal Cards)
- "Selectivity First" with a funnel icon, "Index Design" with a lightning icon, "Bounded Queries" with a shield icon

Section 2: "THE COST" (Middle Row, Side-by-Side Comparison)
- Left Box (Red Theme): "NON-SELECTIVE — Full Table Scan". Shows a red status pill "DANGER — ANTI-PATTERN".
- Right Box (Green Theme): "SELECTIVE — Index Path, Bounded Results". Shows a green status pill "SUCCESS — BEST PRACTICE".

Section 3: "CODE PATTERNS" (Main Center)
- Left Column (Bad Code in Red Border):
  - Heading: "// BAD"
  - Code block:
    SELECT Id, Subject, Description, Body__c
FROM Case
WHERE Status != 'Closed'
AND Subject LIKE '%refund%'
// Scans 8M+ rows — no index hit
// 92% of rows match Status != Closed
- Right Column (Good Code in Green Border):
  - Heading: "// GOOD"
  - Code block:
    SELECT Id, CaseNumber, Priority
FROM Case
WHERE AccountId = :accountId
AND CreatedDate = LAST_N_DAYS:90
AND Type = :caseType
LIMIT 100
// Indexed path → fast, bounded

Section 4: "DEVELOPER PROFILE & CHECKLIST" (Bottom Row)
- Left Side: A 3D Pixar-style cartoon avatar portrait of a dark-skinned South Asian male with a full thick black beard, shoulder-length wavy dark hair pulled up into a messy top bun, warm friendly smile showing teeth, wearing a grey crew-neck sweatshirt with the word "MAGNETIC" on it, set against a warm orange bokeh background. The avatar is labeled "Bennie Joseph | Salesforce Architect". Next to it is a quote bubble: "Architecting intuitive systems, engineering scalable solutions."
- Right Side: A checklist with points: "Filter on indexed fields: AccountId, CreatedDate, External_Id__c", "Avoid leading wildcards — LIKE '%term%' kills index usage", "Never use != on high-cardinality fields like Status", "Constrain date ranges — unbounded scans collapse at LDV scale", "Run Query Plan in Workbench before deploying to production".
- Footer: A thin bar with icons for "Selectivity", "Index Path", "Query Plan", "Data Modeling" and the text "CODE SMART. AUTOMATE FASTER. DELIVER EXCELLENCE."

Style Details: Super high resolution, technical drawing grid, razor-sharp white and neon text, crisp layout, developer IDE aesthetic, flat vector graphics with subtle glow, highly legible monospace font for code blocks, professional presentation.
images: Dark blueprint developer infographic: 'SOQL Optimization: Selectivity First'. Deep dark navy background, glowing neon blue header 'SOQL Optimization: Selectivity First'. Subtitle 'Fast SOQL filters candidate rows before sorting, joining, or rendering'. TOP ROW: 3 horizontal cards — 'Selective Filters with funnel icon', 'Indexed Fields with lightning index icon', 'Bounded Results with LIMIT 200 icon'. MIDDLE: side-by-side — LEFT red-themed box 'NON-SELECTIVE QUERY' with red DANGER pill, RIGHT green-themed box 'SELECTIVE QUERY' with green SUCCESS pill. CENTER: left column red border '// BAD' code: WHERE Status != 'Closed' AND Subject LIKE '%refund%', right column green border '// GOOD' code: WHERE AccountId = :accountId AND CreatedDate = LAST_N_DAYS:90 AND Type = :caseType LIMIT 100. BOTTOM: circular avatar 'Bennie Joseph | Architect' + checklist 'Filter on indexed fields', 'Avoid leading wildcards', 'Constrain date ranges'. Footer bar: 'If Salesforce scans too many rows, Apex cannot rescue the design.'. Monospace font for code, razor-sharp neon text, grid lines.

Dark blueprint developer infographic: 'SOQL Anti-Patterns vs Production Patterns'. Deep dark navy background, glowing neon blue header 'SOQL Anti-Patterns vs Production Patterns'. Subtitle 'Bulkified code can still be slow when filters are non-selective'. TOP ROW: 3 horizontal cards — 'Avoid SOQL in Loops with loop warning icon', 'Avoid SELECT Everything with payload icon', 'Avoid Fuzzy LIKE with magnifying glass icon'. MIDDLE: side-by-side — LEFT red-themed box 'ANTI-PATTERNS' with red DANGER pill, RIGHT green-themed box 'PRODUCTION PATTERNS' with green SUCCESS pill. CENTER: left column red border '// BAD' code: SELECT Id, Description__c FROM Claim__c WHERE Status__c != 'Archived' AND Description__c LIKE '%text%', right column green border '// GOOD' code: SELECT Id, Claim_Number__c FROM Claim__c WHERE Policy__c = :policyId AND Loss_Date__c = LAST_N_DAYS:730 LIMIT 100. BOTTOM: circular avatar 'Bennie Joseph | Architect' + checklist 'Use Query Plan', 'Design indexed lookup fields', 'Split list and detail queries'. Footer bar: 'Optimization is a data access design problem, not a syntax cleanup task.'. Monospace font for code, razor-sharp neon text, grid lines.
